title: Hieracium kemulariae
taxonomy: Hieracium kemulariae is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants that produce composite flowers with multiple florets. The genus Hieracium, to which Hieracium kemulariae belongs, is a group of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. The species is further categorized as a member of the Hieracium subgenus, which is characterized by its distinctive leaf and stem morphology. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Hieracium kemulariae is closely related to other flowering plants that share similar characteristics.

intro: Hieracium kemulariae, also known as Kemularia hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and wounds. Bioactive constituents of Hieracium kemulariae include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
